front: Concept: measures development by what people can do rather than what they have; introduced in the Tanner Lecture 'Equality of What?'
Philosopher: identified personal, social, and environmental conversion factors as limits to a framework for measuring development
Philosopher: extended Rawls's concern for primary goods to 'what goods do to human beings' in developing a welfare economics framework
Concept: seeks to equate distributive justice with establishing a level of 'midfare' for all members of society
Concept: demonstrates that Pareto optimality is incompatible with minimal liberalism, illustrated with a 'lewd' and 'prude' agent example
work: The Liberal Paradox (Collective Choice and Social Welfare)

front: Philosopher: channeled Arrow's Impossibility Theorem to show markets cannot be both efficient and respect freedom of choice
work: The Liberal Paradox (Collective Choice and Social Welfare)

front: Work: argued that the 1943 Bengal famine was caused by panic buying and price rises rather than a lack of food supply, killing 3 million
work: Poverty and Famines / Bengal Famine Analysis

front: Philosopher: argued famines result from distribution failures — hoarding, panic buying, price gouging — not from insufficient food production
work: Poverty and Famines / Bengal Famine Analysis

front: Work: NYRB article estimating the male-biased sex ratio in Asia accounts for more than 100 million 'missing' women
back: More Than 100 Million Women Are Missing (Amartya Sen)
work: More Than 100 Million Women Are Missing

front: Philosopher: Emily Oster disputed this thinker's figure of 100 million in the 'missing women problem'
work: More Than 100 Million Women Are Missing

front: Philosopher: argued in The Idea of Justice that Adam Smith's impartial spectator is a better alternative to the veil of ignorance
Philosopher: wrote an introduction to Adam Smith's A Theory of Moral Sentiments, emphasizing Smith's theories must be read in the context of his moral philosophy
Philosopher: worked with Marxist supervisor Maurice Dobb on a principle that when investment share is sub-optimal, it is optimal to invest in capital-intensive projects
Philosopher: co-authored a report with Joseph Stiglitz criticizing a focus on GDP as a measure of development
Philosopher: discussed reflecting on prices at a local fish market as a childhood influence in the 2022 memoir Home in the World
